result.summary: Figma publicly advertises developer surfaces for plugins, widgets, a REST API, and a SCIM API; the corpus only provides concrete authentication details for an OAuth-protected MCP auth flow, so the REST and SCIM surfaces are present but their auth is not determinable from the corpus.

figma_mcp_oauth_client.label: Figma MCP OAuth client/session
figma_mcp_oauth_client.setup: Use Figma's OAuth flow for MCP with authorization at [Figma](https://figma.com) via [`https://www.figma.com/oauth/mcp`](https://www.figma.com/oauth/mcp). The detected flow uses token exchange at [`https://api.figma.com/v1/oauth/token`](https://api.figma.com/v1/oauth/token) and supports the `mcp:connect` scope. Dynamic client registration is available at [`https://api.figma.com/v1/oauth/mcp/register`](https://api.figma.com/v1/oauth/mcp/register).

surfaces.name: Figma REST API
surfaces.notes: The corpus says Figma has a REST API for external tools and products, but does not include request-level authentication details.

surfaces.name: Figma SCIM API
surfaces.notes: The corpus explicitly advertises a SCIM API for account provisioning, but does not provide an endpoint or authentication details.
